返回本批次

太阳能阳台席卷欧洲

Solar Balconies Take Europe by Storm

hackaday.com 作者 lxm 分数 88 评论 36 2026/04/02 10:17
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 太阳能已经存在很长时间了。曾几何时，这里主要是研究项目和大型市政设施的保护区。最终，随着这项技术变得越来越便宜，屋顶太阳能出现了，有钱的房主争先恐后地在家里安装太阳能电池板，以削减电费，在某些情况下甚至还能赚钱。那些住在公寓或出租房里的人基本上被排除在太阳能革命之外。直到阳台太阳能的出现。这一概念在德国很受欢迎，但在世界其他地方却鲜为人知，它已将家庭发电带入了比以往更大的市场。
  2. 很久以前，光伏太阳能电池板的制造成本非常昂贵。这使得太阳能难以与化石燃料等传统能源竞争。即使来自太阳本身的能源实际上是免费的，高昂的安装成本和有限的电力输出也使商业案例变得困难。然而，随着多年来对清洁能源的需求不断增加，太阳能电池板的产量也随之增加。规模经济发挥了作用，面板变得足够便宜，以至于个人可以考虑将其安装在自己的家中。这导致了屋顶太阳能的广泛采用，安装功率通常在 5 kW 至 10 kW 范围内，并配有逆变器硬件，以便以安全和受控的方式将能量反馈回电网。德语中的常用术语是“balkonkraftwerk”，翻译过来就是“阳台发电厂”。然而，实际上并不需要阳台。如果您能找到一种方法将电线连接到电源点，那么您可以将它们挂在建筑物外的任何地方都可以。图片来源：RobbieIanMorrison，CC BY 4.0 屋顶太阳能的问题在于并非每个人都拥有自己的屋顶。世界各地有很多人住在公寓或租房里，无法对他们的家进行永久性的调整。这些群体基本上被排除在太阳能革命之外。直到太阳能电池板变得如此便宜并且电费变得足够高，以至于即使是小规模的安装也开始具有经济意义。特别是在德国，小型太阳能装置已经相当流行，该国已成为所谓“阳台”太阳能装置的温床。这些涉及一两个太阳能电池板的简单设置，这些太阳能电池板被设计为可以轻松安装在阳台或家庭的其他户外区域，而不是永久安装在屋顶上。它们配备了小型逆变器，可将太阳能电池板的直流输出转换为交流电，然后直接插入现有的家庭电源插座。这种自助安装方法无需雇用电工，进一步提高了系统的经济性。这些系统使用的逆变器包括防孤岛保护功能，这样，如果电网因维修或维修而断电，太阳能系统就不会为任何电路供电。与屋顶安装相比，阳台太阳能确实有一些局限性。通常，安装角度不适合充分利用阳光。从这样的系统中获得的功率也存在限制。德国针对“balkonkraftwerk”系统的最初规定规定，馈电功率必须限制在 600 瓦，以避免家庭布线和插座出现潜在问题，而这些家庭布线和插座从未设计用于馈电太阳能。更新后的规定允许高达 800 瓦的馈入功率，并附加规定安装的面板不得超过 2000 瓦的峰值输出水平。这听起来可能有点不匹配，但当输出超过 800 瓦限制时，可以使用面板的多余电力为电池充电。当阳光不那么明亮时，拥有更高峰值输出的更大面板也很有用。一组 2000 瓦峰值面板设置将输出 800 瓦或更高的功率，远高于一组在峰值条件下仅提供 800 瓦的面板。面板通常以临时方式安装。图片来源：Nikolai Twin，CC BY 4.0 尽管存在这些限制，或者可能正因为这些限制，在德国发展太阳能还是轻而易举的事。只需前往当地的大型商店，购买一套套件，然后将其挂在房子的一侧即可。一旦你把它插入墙上，你就大功告成了。大多数套件都附带某种用于监控系统的应用程序，因此您可以密切关注面板的发电量。由于交通便利，安装量激增，该国已有超过 100 万个阳台太阳能装置投入运行。到目前为止，阳台太阳能主要是德国人的发明。然而，世界其他地区正在迎头赶上。西班牙、法国和比利时等其他欧洲国家都已经登上了这趟列车。在美国，犹他州已经批准了阳台太阳能安装框架，弗吉尼亚州紧随其后。关键是制定特殊措施，以便为小型太阳能系统提供简单、廉价的 DIY 安装。通常，在大多数州安装屋顶太阳能需要与当地公用事业公司签署有关向电网供电的协议，并聘请专业承包商进行安装。这增加了巨大的成本，而小型太阳能系统永远无法在合理的时间内收回成本。通过消除小规模插件设置的这些障碍，它们变得可行并且更容易被更多的社区所访问。阳台太阳能套件在德国各地的商店里都很容易买到。 800 瓦的套件仅需几百欧元。图片来源：Lidl，截图
  3. 阳台太阳能不太可能立即改变游戏规则，彻底改变电网。大多数安装都是低功耗的。他们的电力大部分被吸收来运行一台冰箱和一两台电视，很少有人为更广泛的电网做出重大贡献。然而，它们在欧洲的受欢迎程度表明，更广泛的社区非常渴望以任何方式或方式登上太阳能列车。至少，阳台太阳能是一个巨大的商机，它比以往任何时候都将可持续发展带到城市和郊区景观的更多角落。特色图片：[Triplec85] 的“Sogenanntes Balkonkraftwerk”

Original Summary

  1. Solar power has been around for a long time now. Once upon a time, it was mostly the preserve of research projects and large-scale municipal installations. Eventually, as the technology grew ever cheaper, rooftop solar came along, and cashed-up homeowners rushed to throw panels on their homes to slash their power bills and even make money in some cases. Those in apartments or rented accommodations had largely been left out of the solar revolution. That was, until the advent of balcony solar. Popular in Germany, but little known in the rest of the world, the concept has brought home power generation to a larger market than ever.
  2. Pop One On Photovoltaic solar panels were very expensive to manufacture, a long time ago. This made it difficult for solar power to compete with traditional energy sources like fossil fuels. High install costs and limited power output made the business case difficult, even if the energy from the sun itself was effectively free. However, as the desire for cleaner sources of energy ramped up over the years, solar panel production ramped up in turn. Economies of scale did their thing, and panels grew cheap enough for individuals to consider installing them on their own homes. This led to the widespread uptake of rooftop solar, with installations commonly in the 5 kW to 10 kW range with inverter hardware to allow feeding energy back into the grid in a safe and controlled manner. The common term is “balkonkraftwerk” in German, which translates to “balcony power plant.” However, there’s no real need for a balcony. Anywhere you can hang them outside a building will work if you can find a way to route the wires to a power point. Credit: RobbieIanMorrison, CC BY 4.0 The problem with rooftop solar is that not everybody owns their roof. A great many people around the world live in apartments, or rent, and are not in a position to make permanent adjustments to their home. These groups were largely left out of the solar revolution. That was, until solar panels grew so cheap and power bills grew high enough that even small-scale installs started to make financial sense. In Germany in particular, small solar installs have become quite popular, and the country has become a hotbed for so-called “balcony” solar installations. These involve simple setups of one or two solar panels which are designed to be easily mounted on a balcony or other outdoor area of a home, rather than permanently installed on a rooftop. They come with small inverters to convert the DC output of the solar panels into AC power, which plug straight into an existing home power socket. This do-it-yourself install method eliminates the need for hiring an electrician, further improving the affordability of the system. The inverters used with these systems include anti-islanding protection so that the solar system does not power any circuits if the grid has been deenergized for service or repair. Balcony solar does have some limitations compared to rooftop installs. Often, installation angles are imperfect for making the most of the sun available. There are also limitations to how much power you can get out of such a system. Germany’s initial regulations for “balkonkraftwerk” systems stated that feed in power had to be limited to 600 watts to avoid potential issues with household wiring and sockets that were never designed for feed-in solar power. Updated regulations allow up to 800 watts of feed-in, with an additional regulation that the installed panels do not exceed a level of 2000 watts peak output. It might sound like a mismatch, but it’s possible to use the excess power from the panels to charge a battery when the output exceeds the 800-watt limit. Having larger panels with higher peak output is useful, too, for when the sun isn’t shining so bright. A 2000 watt peak panel setup will be outputting 800 watts or more far more often than a set of panels that only delivers 800 watts in peak conditions. The panels are generally installed in ad-hoc fashion. Credit: Nikolai Twin, CC BY 4.0 Despite the limitations, or perhaps because of them, it’s a cinch to get yourself going with solar in Germany. Just head to your local big box store, purchase a kit, and hang it off the side of your house. Once you plug it in to the wall, you’re pretty much done. Most kits come with some sort of app for monitoring the system so you can keep an eye on how much your panels are generating. The ease of access has led to an explosion in installs, with over 1 million balcony solar setups already operating in the country. Thus far, balcony solar has been largely a German thing. However, other parts of the world are catching on. Other European nations like Spain, France, and Belgium have all got on board the train already. In the United States, the state of Utah has already approved a framework for balcony solar installs, and Virginia is following close behind. The key has been carving out special measures to allow easy, cheap DIY installs for small solar systems. Typically, setting up rooftop solar in most states requires signing an agreement with the local utility regarding power feed-in to the grid, as well as hiring professional contractors for the installation. This adds a huge amount of cost which a small solar system would never recoup in a reasonable amount of time. By eliminating these hurdles for small-scale plug-in setups, they become viable and far more accessible to more of the community. Balcony solar kits are readily available at stores across Germany. An 800-watt kit can be had for as little as a few hundred Euros. Credit: Lidl via screenshot
  3. Balcony solar is unlikely to be an instant gamechanger that drastically shakes up the power grid. Most installs are low power. Their juice is mostly sucked up to run a fridge and a TV or two, and few make major feed-in contributions to the broader grid. However, their popularity in Europe shows that there is a serious eagerness amongst the broader community to get on board the solar train any which way or how. At the very least, balcony solar is a grand business opportunity and one that is bringing sustainability to more corners of the urban and suburban landscape than ever before. Featured image: “Sogenanntes Balkonkraftwerk” by [Triplec85]

评论（默认中文）

adrianN 2026/04/02 12:20
我的阳台上有 2kW 的面板和 4kWh 的电池。我对这个设置很满意。我预计它会在短短几年内收回成本。我唯一希望它有开放的 API 来控制逆变器和电池，最好是通过蓝牙，这样我就不会被迫使用应用程序。
I have 2kW of panels on my balcony and 4kWh of batteries. I'm happy with the setup. I expect it to pay for itself in just a few years. The only thing I wish it had is open APIs to control the inverter and the batteries, ideally over bluetooth, so that I'm not forced to use an app.
在 HN 中查看
effdee 2026/04/02 12:36
如果端口 502/TCP 已打开，您可能可以通过 Modbus 协议访问它。实现 Modbus 客户端很简单。

我的重新命名的 Fox ESS 硬件已启用它，甚至还有所谓的“寄存器”的官方文档。

If port 502/TCP is open you can probably access it via Modbus protocol. Implementing a Modbus client is trivial.

My rebranded Fox ESS hardware has it enabled and there's even official documentation of the so-called "registers".

xigurat 2026/04/02 15:45
Solakon One 有一个相当不错的 API 您可以在不涉及云的情况下进行控制......
Solakon One has a quite decent API you can control without cloud being involved...

herbst 2026/04/02 15:26
我使用 victron 设备和锂电池 + 您选择的 esp32 以及一些 LLM 魔法来读取数据并将其从蓝牙转发到网络。

对于 victron 和 Litime 来说，都有大量示例，包括家庭助理集成。

I use victron devices and a litime battery + an esp32 of your choice and some LLM magic to read and relay data from Bluetooth to web.

For both victron and litime plenty of examples exist, including home assistant integrations.

Hehe "Balkonkraftwerk", available from Lidl for €250 (see TFA). This makes me unreasonably happy for some reason.

testing22321 2026/04/02 11:23
我真的希望这些在加拿大变得合法。

现在看来犹他州是北美唯一有他们所在的司法管辖区

I really hope these become legal in Canada.

Right now it seems Utah is the only jurisdiction in North America where they are

在 HN 中查看
rickydroll 2026/04/02 21:39
阳台太阳能有两种类型。一种是双向功率流，即经典的阳台太阳能，另一种称为“零输出”，其中如果您开始反转电流并输出功率，则电源上的电流方向传感器会限制逆变器。

在我居住的美国，国家电网对阳台太阳能没问题，只要它是零出口阳台太阳能。您的电力公司可能会采取类似的方法。他们也可能会采取“如果它没有越过仪表，我们无法告诉你在做什么，那么我们就不能告诉你不要这样做”

There are two types of balcony solar. One is bidirectional power flow, i.e., classical balcony solar, and the other is called "zero export", in which a current flow direction sensor on your mains throttles back your inverter if you start to reverse the current flow and export power.

Where I live, in the US, National Grid is okay with balcony solar as long as it's a zero-export balcony solar. Your power utility may take a similar approach. They might also take a "if it doesn't cross the meter, and we can't tell what you're doing, then we can't tell you not to do it"

在 HN 中查看
gizmo686 2026/04/02 23:44
任何需要电工来修改您的电源连接（随后可能是市政检查）的事情都违背了阳台太阳能的主要优点，即它是一种商品单元，可以由非专家安装，无需任何繁文缛节。

此外，出于安全考虑，公用事业公司不需要切断电源。他们关心的安全不是新的电流回流；而是新的电流回流。而是电流回流到未通电的电网中。并网逆变器不会这样做。如果电网出现故障，它们会自行关闭，无需上游关闭。

如果公用事业公司的计量表使得回流强制进行净计量，则其可能对回流有合理的业务目标。在这里，这不是问题，因为网络计量无论如何都是住宅连接的法律。即使在净计量不是法律的司法管辖区，我也不认为这令人信服。阳台太阳能的容量有限意味着它实际上不会大量发生，如果它确实成为问题，他们可以承担升级计量设备的成本。

Anything that requires an electrician to come and modify your mains connections (followed, presumably by a municipal inspection), defeats the main benefit of balcony solar, which is that it is a commodity unit that can be installed by non-experts without any red tape.

Further, the utility's safety concerns do not require any shut off on the mains. Their safty concern is not a new backflow of current; but a backflow of current into an otherwise non-energized grid. Grid-tied inverters will not do this. If the grid goes down, they shut themselves down without any need for an upstream shutoff.

The utility's may have a reasonable business object to back-flow if their meters are such that backflow forces net-metering. Around here, that is a non-issue because net-metering is the law for residential connections anyway. Even in juristictions where net-metering is not the law, I don't find this convincing. The limited capacity of balcony solar means that it won't actually happen in any significant amount, and if it does become a problem, they can shoulder the cost of upgrading their metering equipment.

在 HN 中查看
bigthymer 2026/04/02 17:44

bamboozled 2026/04/02 11:28
我对这一切感到唯一消极的是我们现在正在做的事情。一旦冰川消失，降雪就会消失，大规模的死亡就会开始。他们说迟做总比不做好，但我们为什么不在 90 年代就投资这个呢？
The only negative thing I feel about all of this is that we're doing now. Once the glaciers are farked, the snow is going and the mass die offs are started. Better late than never they say, but why the hell didn't we just invest in this in the 1990s?

bamboozled 2026/04/02 12:50

Tade0 2026/04/02 16:31
德国确实在朝这个方向前进，但中国的制造规模才促使人们引入数百项降低成本所需的小改动。
They were and Germany was actually going in that direction, but it took the manufacturing scale of China to create an incentive to introduce the hundreds of minor changes necessary to get costs down.

harvey9 2026/04/03 01:07

That's not a contradiction. From the point of view of the factory or household consuming it, it is cheap.
在 HN 中查看
muskstinks 2026/04/02 19:41
德国停止了投资并扼杀了太阳能产业。并不是说中国也提供了帮助，但我们不知道如果没有中国，德国是否能够渡过难关。
germany stoped the investment and killed the solar industry. Not saying that China helped too, but we don't know if germany would have pushed through it without China.
在 HN 中查看
Tade0 2026/04/02 23:22
这只是在中国开始吃他们（和日本）的午餐之后才发生的。有一个很棒的旧图表显示了这一点：

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:5_Largest_Producers_...

就在那时，德国的太阳能热潮正式开始：

https://www.statista.com/chart/2397/solar-power-made-massive...

其中主要由中国面板提供动力。由于我们不能拥有好东西，所以征收了关税：

https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_13_...

That only happened after China started eating their (and Japan's) lunch. There's a great old chart which shows this:

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:5_Largest_Producers_...

Right around that time Germany's solar boom started in earnest:

https://www.statista.com/chart/2397/solar-power-made-massive...

Which was powered largely by Chinese panels. Since we can't have nice things, tariffs were imposed:

https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_13_...

在 HN 中查看
GoToRO 2026/04/02 13:34
其他卖能源的人的大厅：不是绿色类型。他们有钱、有律师、有广告，什么都有。由于能源短缺，现在只允许阳台太阳能发电。
Lobby from the other people selling energy: the not green type. They have the money, the lawyers, the ads, everything. Balcony solar power is only allowed now due to energy shortage.
在 HN 中查看
adrianN 2026/04/02 19:33
德国多年来一直允许阳台太阳能。
Balcony solar has been allowed for a number of years in Germany.
在 HN 中查看
acdha 2026/04/02 20:14
我们确实从 20 世纪 70 年代开始进行了投资，这也是导致价格大幅下降的原因。只是需要时间才能使研发达到具有成本竞争力的程度。

社会问题在于，化石燃料的使用非常有利可图，并且雇用了足够多的人，以至于污染者可以不用为外部性买单，而事实证明，由石油公司资助的大量否认气候变化的言论，有效地让政客和公众淡化了风险。即使在今天，即使在 HN 上，你也会发现有人会说这没什么大不了的，地球过去一直这么温暖，这会花费太多，他们永远无法驾驶电动汽车，因为他们每天的通勤时间是单程 11 小时不停歇，等等。

由于化石燃料游说者如此成功地赢得了大多数右翼政党的支持，数百万人将这一点添加到他们的自我认同中，因此很难承认自己错了，因为这或多或少意味着承认在气候问题上对他们撒谎的人也在其他​​事情上撒了谎。

We did invest, starting in the 1970s, which is what brought the prices down so much. It just took time to get the R&D to the point where they were cost competitive.

The social problem is that fossil fuel usage was very profitable and employed enough people that polluters were allowed not to pay for externalities and a lot of climate change denial funded by the oil companies proved effective at getting politicians and the public to downplay the risk. Even today, even on HN, you can find people who’ll say it’s no big deal, the earth has been this warm in the past, that it’ll cost too much, that they can never drive an EV because their daily commute is 11 hours each way without stopping, etc.

Because fossil fuel lobbyists so successfully captured most right-wing parties, millions of people added that to their self identity and thus will struggle to admit they were wrong because that more or less means admitting that the same people who lied to them about the climate also lied about other things.

mudil 2026/04/02 13:04
这应该是欧洲的一项革命性发明吗？
Is this supposed to be a revolutionary European invention?
在 HN 中查看
WithinReason 2026/04/02 14:21
“1839 年，法国物理学家埃德蒙·贝克勒尔 (Edmond Becquerel) 首次观察到某些材料通过光照产生电荷的能力”

所以是的

"In 1839, the ability of some materials to create an electrical charge from light exposure was first observed by the French physicist Edmond Becquerel"

So yes

在 HN 中查看
widdershins 2026/04/02 14:20
标题和文章都没有暗示这一点。
Neither the title nor the article implied so.
在 HN 中查看
kkfx 2026/04/02 17:59
所有这些都是为了否认新政与密集城市不相容的事实。抱歉，但算一下，你就会发现未来是住宅和棚屋，而不是公寓。
All to deny the fact that new deal is incompatible with dense cities. Sorry but do the math and you realize that the future is homes and sheds, not condos.
在 HN 中查看
muskstinks 2026/04/02 19:43
什么“新政”？如果你谈论太阳能和光伏发电，它所增加的价值已经与价值的多少无关。即使是人口稠密的城市也有屋顶。他们还有高速公路，旁边有足够的直接空间，没有人愿意居住或种植农产品（由于污染）
What 'new deal'? If you talk about Solar and PV, it adds value already independen of how little it is. Even dense city has roofs on top. Also they have highways which have plenty direct space next to them were no one wants to live or grow produce (due to polution)
在 HN 中查看
SoftTalker 2026/04/02 11:22
> 它们配备小型逆变器，可将太阳能电池板的直流输出转换为交流电，然后直接插入现有的家庭电源插座。

希望这些逆变器足够智能，能够在交流主电源中断时切断馈电，以避免反馈可能正在维修的公用设施线路。

> They come with small inverters to convert the DC output of the solar panels into AC power, which plug straight into an existing home power socket.

Hopefully these inverters are smart enough to cut the feed if the AC mains power goes out, to avoid backfeeding utility lines that may be under repair.

在 HN 中查看
jacquesm 2026/04/02 13:00
您无法购买经过认证但不具备此功能的逆变器。除此之外，还有一整套其他安全措施和电网质量措施。

例如参见：

https://www.netbeheernederland.nl/sites/default/files/2024-0...

每个地区都有自己的一套规则，要求逆变器制造商根据逆变器的安装位置进行一系列不同的设置。

You can't buy an inverter that is certified that doesn't do this. As well as a whole raft of other safety measures and grid quality measures besides.

See for instance:

https://www.netbeheernederland.nl/sites/default/files/2024-0...

Every region has their own set of rules which requires inverter manufacturers to have a bunch of different settings depending on where the inverter is installed.

在 HN 中查看
couchand 2026/04/02 11:49
幸运的是，他们做到了，事实上这篇文章已经清楚地表明了这一点。 +1 阅读到所引用段落的末尾。
Fortunately they do, and in fact the article makes that clear. +1 for reading to the end of the paragraph that was quoted.
在 HN 中查看
InvisibleUp 2026/04/02 11:39
是的。任何符合 UL 3700（或更普遍的 IEEE 1547 / UL 1741）标准的系统都要求通过在电网断电后两秒内关闭电源来实现反孤岛效应。
Yes. Any system that’s UL 3700 (or more generally IEEE 1547 / UL 1741) compliant mandates anti-islanding by shutting off the power within two seconds of grid loss.
在 HN 中查看
telotortium 2026/04/02 11:33
我认为这就是为什么它们应该被限制在 800 W，但是当整个公寓楼或社区都充满这些设备时，这是否足以避免对公用事业工人造成严重危险？
I think this is why they're supposed to be limited to 800 W, but is that enough to avoid serious danger to utility workers when a whole apartment building or neighborhood is full of these?
在 HN 中查看
namibj 2026/04/02 13:05
中欧有更多埋在地下的电力系统，没有什么是安全的，除非它主动接地，如此牢固，以至于在你感觉到明显的刺痛之前，那个破坏你LOTO的白痴就会电弧闪光。

800W 是关于电网管理影响限制的水平，不保证公用事业公司施加任何“但我们首先必须升级变电站，然后才能为您提供具有更高速电动汽车充电和 McMansion 冬季全热泵设置的本地变压器”延迟，然后才允许您打开它/并网它。

Central Europe has more buried electrical systems and nothing is safe until it's actively grounded so hard it'll arc flash the idiot who broke you LOTO before you even feel a clear tingle.

The 800W is about grid management impact limitation to levels that do not warrant the utility imposing any "but we first have to upgrade the substation before we can get you your local transformer with the higher speed EV charging and McMansion winter full heat pump setup" delays before you are allowed to turn it on/grid-tie it.

在 HN 中查看
adrianN 2026/04/02 13:06
它们仅限于预防火灾。它们位于断路器后面，因此它们输入的任何电力都会在断路器跳闸之前允许电缆上有更多电流。
They are limited to prevent fires. They sit behind the breakers so any power they feed in allows more current on the cables before the breakers trip.
在 HN 中查看
